name: Notre-Dame de Piétat Hills and Gave Loop
name_original: De Gan vers Notre Dame de Pietat
description: This moderate 19.2-mile mountain bike trail from De Gan to Notre Dame de Pietat offers Pyrenees views and requires advanced riding skills.
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Gan are situated in the foothills of the Pyrénées, offering varied terrain for cyclists. The region features rolling hills, dense forests, and river valleys, providing a mix of ascents and descents. Gan mountain biking terrain includes paths through the Piémont Ossalois, characterized by its undulating landscape and scenic views. Best mountain bike trails around Gan The most popular mountain bike trail is Notre-Dame de Piétat Hills and Gave Loop, a 19.2 miles (30.9 km) trail that takes 2 hours 36 minutes to complete. This moderate route features significant elevation gain through the local hills and along the Gave river. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is View of the Piémont Ossalois loop from La Croix de Buzy, an easy 6.7 miles (10.9 km) path. This route offers panoramic views of the Piémont Ossalois landscape. Local mountain bikers also love the Forest trail loop from Gan, a 16.2 miles (26.2 km) trail leading through wooded areas, often completed in about 1 hour 29 minutes. Mountain biking around Gan is defined by its hilly terrain, forest paths, and riverine landscapes. The network offers options for easy rides through woodlands as well as more challenging routes with notable elevation changes. The routes in Gan are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 500 reviews. More than 1800 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Gan's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are there around Gan?
A: There are over 15 mountain bike trails around Gan, offering a variety of experiences. The region features a mix of easy, moderate, and difficult routes to explore.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails near Gan?
A: The mountain bike trails around Gan are situated in the foothills of the Pyrénées, offering varied terrain. You can expect rolling hills, dense forests, and river valleys, providing a mix of ascents and descents. Paths often go through the Piémont Ossalois, known for its undulating landscape and scenic views.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners around Gan?
A: Yes, Gan offers several trails suitable for beginners. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/41588807" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Forest trail loop from Gan&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 16.2-mile (26.2 km) route that leads through wooded areas, perfect for those new to mountain biking.

Q: Are there challenging mountain bike trails for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. While many routes are moderate, there are also more challenging options. The region's hilly terrain and notable elevation changes on some routes will provide a good test for experienced mountain bikers.

Q: What are some scenic viewpoints or natural features to look out for on the trails?
A: Many trails offer panoramic views, especially those traversing the Piémont Ossalois. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20520693" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Piémont Ossalois loop from La Croix de Buzy&lt;/a&gt; provides stunning vistas of the landscape. You'll also encounter riverine landscapes along routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e969758318" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Notre-Dame de Piétat Hills and Gave Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in Gan?
A: Yes, several trails are suitable for families, particularly the easier routes that wind through woodlands with less significant elevation changes. These provide a pleasant experience for riders of varying skill levels.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Gan?
A: The best time for mountain biking in Gan, given its location in the Pyrénées foothills, is typically from spring through autumn. During these seasons, the weather is generally more favorable, and the trails are in good condition. Winter riding might be possible but could be affected by colder temperatures or precipitation.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails that form a loop around Gan?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike trails around Gan are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e969758318" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Notre-Dame de Piétat Hills and Gave Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/41588807" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Forest trail loop from Gan&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Gan?
A: The mountain bike routes in Gan are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 500 reviews. More than 1800 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Gan's varied terrain, often praising the diverse landscapes and well-maintained paths.

Q: Are there any nearby attractions I can visit after my ride?
A: Yes, there are several interesting attractions around Gan. You could visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2185442" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gave de Pau Cycle Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4119926" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;National Museum and Estate of the Château de Pau&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5354510" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pau whitewater stadium&lt;/a&gt;. These offer a mix of historical and recreational points of interest.

Q: How long are the mountain bike trails around Gan?
A: The mountain bike trails around Gan vary in length. You can find routes ranging from shorter, easy loops like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20520194" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Piémont Ossalois loop from La Croix de Buzy&lt;/a&gt; at about 6.7 miles (10.8 km), to longer, more challenging rides such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e969758318" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Notre-Dame de Piétat Hills and Gave Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is 19.2 miles (30.9 km) long.

Q: Are there places to park near the mountain bike trails in Gan?
A: While specific parking locations are not detailed for every trail, Gan is a well-established area. You can generally find parking in or near the town centers or designated trailheads, especially for popular routes. It's advisable to check local maps or komoot tour details for specific starting points.

A lot of beautiful paths for mountain biking around!

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the De Gan vers Notre Dame de Pietat mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: This trail is rated as a moderate mountain bike ride. It has a technical difficulty rating of 3.5 on the Single Track Scale (STS), corresponding to an S2 level. This means it includes some technical sections and requires advanced riding skills.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied and presents technical challenges, consistent with its moderate difficulty rating. You'll likely encounter dirt roads and sections within wooded areas, featuring both ascents and descents.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The primary destination and highlight is the beautiful Notre-Dame de Piétat, known for its sublime decor. Along the way, you can also enjoy a View of the Pyrenees and Béarn from Route de Piétat, offering panoramic vistas of the Pays de Nay and the initial peaks of the Pyrenees.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the De Gan vers Notre Dame de Pietat mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: The route spans approximately 30.9 kilometers with an elevation gain of 730 meters. Riders can expect to complete it in about 2 hours and 37 minutes, depending on their pace and skill level.

faqs.question: Is the De Gan vers Notre Dame de Pietat trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this trail is rated as moderate with technical sections requiring advanced riding skills. It is not recommended for beginners.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: While not explicitly stated, moderate mountain biking trails are generally best enjoyed in spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Avoid extreme summer heat or winter conditions which can make the trail more challenging or less enjoyable.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the De Gan vers Notre Dame de Pietat mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are allowed on mountain biking trails in France, but they should always be kept under control, preferably on a leash, to ensure the safety of wildlife, other trail users, and your pet. Please clean up after your dog.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay entrance fees for this mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: No specific permits or entrance fees are typically required for public mountain biking trails in this region of France. However, always respect local regulations and private property signs.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the De Gan vers Notre Dame de Pietat trail?
faqs.answer: Parking is generally available in or near the town of Gan, which serves as a common starting point for the route. Look for designated parking areas or consult local maps for the most convenient access points.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with any other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route briefly intersects with sections of the Véloroute de la vallée d'Ossau / Chemin de Saint Jacques Voie d'Arles and the Voie Verte de la Vallée d'Ossau.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or places to eat near the trail?
faqs.answer: While the trail itself is in a natural setting, the nearby towns of Gan and Pardies-Piétat offer various options for cafes, pubs, and restaurants where you can refuel after your ride.

waypoints.name: View of the Pyrenees and Béarn from Route de Piétat
